name: Masa Biscuits with Ham and Green Chile Gravy
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Firm, smoky ham, trimmed and cut into 1/4 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. In an open flame or under a pre-heated broiler, roast the long green chiles, turning them until they are lightly but evenly charred.
2. Steam the chiles in a paper bag or covered bowl with a plate until cool. Rub away the burned peel. Stem and seed the chiles and coarsely chop them (about 3/4 cup).
3. In a large skillet over medium-low heat, melt the butter. Add the ham cubes and cook, stirring constantly, until lightly browned about 10 minutes.
4. Sprinkle the flour over the ham cubes and cook, stirring constantly, for 5 minutes.
5. Slowly whisk in the milk into the ham mixture. Stir in the green chiles and salt. Raise the heat to medium and bring the milk mixture to a simmer.
6. Cook, stirring occasionally and scraping the bottom of the pan until thickened into a medium gravy about 7 minutes. Adjust seasoning as needed.
7. Arrange the biscuits, split-sides up, on plates. Spoon the gravy over and around each serving. Serve immediately.
NOTE: For extra heat, garnish with fresh jalepeno slices. This dish can also be made using smoked turkey thigh instead of ham.
